heroHeadline: Gaza Holds First Elections in 20 Years as IDF Strikes Kill 7+ More Palestinians
heroSubtitle: Palestinians voted in the first Gaza municipal elections in 20 years on April 25, with 22.7% turnout in Deir al-Balah while IDF forces simultaneously struck Hamas police on election duty in Sheikh Radwan, Gaza City. On April 26, a wave of Israeli strikes killed at least four more Palestinians — including a drone strike on a motorcycle near Kuwait Roundabout in Gaza City and an air attack at Al-Mughraqa — as a 14-year-old died of earlier injuries. Al Jazeera's ceasefire analysis confirmed the post-October 2025 ceasefire Palestinian death toll has now exceeded 800. Fatah claimed a sweeping victory in the elections on April 26, though Hamas exclusion and uncontested races in major West Bank cities drew criticism.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, IDF, Gaza Ministry of Health, B'Tselem, ICC, ICJ, Reuters, Al Jazeera, OHCHR, UNRWA, Yesh Din, Human Rights Watch, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are subject to ongoing verification. The conflict has persisted since Israeli independence on May 14, 1948.
